retrieved.title: The academic year
retrieved.text:
The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

- The University of Vienna is closed on [public holidays](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/feiertage/).
- [Application and admission periods for degree programmes without an entrance exam procedure](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Application and admission period for degree programmes with an entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/deadlines-for-entrance-exam-procedures/)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 March 2026 |
| Rectors's Day (no classes) | 12 March 2026 |
| Easter break | 30 March to 12 April 2026 |
| Whitsun break | 23 May to 25 May 2026 |
| End of classes | 30 June 2026 |
| Lecture-free period (summer break) | 1 July to 30 September 2026 |
| End of semester | 30 September 2026 |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 October 2026 |
| All Souls Day | 2 November 2026 |
| Christmas break | 21 December 2026 to 6 January 2027 |
| End of classes | 31 January 2027 |
| Lecture-free period (semester break) | 1 to 28 February 2027 |
| End of semester | 28 February 2027 |

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text:
Your second step before starting your studies is the completion of the admission procedure within the admission period.

The admission is only possible if you have applied for admission within the application period and fulfil all admission requirements.

Our tip: Complete the admission procedure as soon as possible. You can only register for courses and exams after you have been admitted to the degree programme of your choice.

- from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- semester start: 1. October

- from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- semester start: 1 March

- [Determination of the admission and registration periods for bachelor, diploma and master programmes as well as for non-degree programmes for winter semester 2025/26 and summer semester 2026 (only available in German)](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2024_2025/2024_2025_31.pdf) (06.12.2024)
- [Determination of the admission and registration periods for bachelor, diploma and master programmes as well as for non-degree programmes for winter semester 2026/27 and summer semester 2027 (only available in German)](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2025_2026/2025_2026_9.pdf) (24.10.2025)
- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 61 to 62](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. For example: You start your bachelor's programme in the winter semester of 2022/2023. You neither interrupt your studies nor take a leave of absence. You must obtain the minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2024 the latest. The deadline is on 31 October 2024. If you take a leave of absence, the semesters during which you are on leave do not count. After the end of your leave of absence, the semesters are counted again until you have studied for four semesters. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all

retrieved.title: Deadlines and dates
retrieved.text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Download Test dates (accessible PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testtermine_AV202627_EN.pdf)

Previous

©

Next

What do you need to study, how does the test day work, and what do you need to keep in mind? We’ve put together all the information you need – so you can successfully get through the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

[Through the entrance exam](/en/through-the-entrance-exam)

retrieved.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different master programme without selection procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. For example: You start your bachelor's programme in the winter semester of 2022/2023. You neither interrupt your studies nor take a leave of absence. You must obtain the minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2024 the latest. The deadline is on 31 October 2024. If you take a leave of absence, the semesters during which you are on leave do not count. After the end of your leave of absence, the semesters are counted again until you have studied for four semesters. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? reranked.title: Deadlines and dates
reranked.text:
- #### Application period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship, once per year:

  - **Academc year 2026/27:**
    2 March to 4 May 2026
- #### Admission Period

  Irrespective of the applicant's citizenship

  - **Winter semester 2026/27**
    13 July to 31 October 2026
  - **Summer semester 2027**
    11 January to 31 March 2027

**When will the written test for my desired degree programme take place?**

Here you will find an overview of all test dates for bachelor's and diploma programmes with entrance exam/aptitude test procedures in the academic year 2026/27.

**My desired degree programme says 'Test doesn't take place.' What does that mean?**

There is no written test because there are fewer applications for admission than there are study places available this year.

The application period for the 2026/27 academic year has already passed. You can only be admitted to the course if you

- submitted your application on time and
- meet all the admission requirements

[Download Test dates (accessible PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/aufnahmeverfahren/Dateien/Testtermine_AV202627_EN.pdf)

What do you need to study, how does the test day work, and what do you need to keep in mind? We’ve put together all the information you need – so you can successfully get through the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

[Through the entrance exam](/en/through-the-entrance-exam)

Previous

©

Next

reranked.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
reranked.text:
The following deadlines apply for EU/EEA/CH citizens and groups of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/tuition-fee/equal-status-with-eueea-citizens/decree-on-certain-groups-of-persons/).

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 5 September 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 5 February 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

The following deadlines apply for citizens of non-EU/EEA countries.

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 22 June to 3 August 2026
- Admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 16 November 2026 to 7 January 2027
- Admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.title: Studying at the University of Vienna again – different degree programme
heading_path: Different master programme without selection procedure
Note for graduates of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The application and admission period for graduates of the University of Vienna who want to study an eligible master programme according to the curriculum for winter semester 2026/27 is from 22 June to 31 October 2026. The application and admission period for summer semester 2027 is from 16 November 2026 to 31 March 2027.

The direct admission to [consecutive master programmes in the same discipline](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/masterstudien/mit-abschluss-der-universitaet-wien/tagesaktuelle-zulassung/) (only available in German) is possible year round for graduates of a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: Application and admission periods
reranked.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

selected.title: The academic year
selected.text:
The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

- The University of Vienna is closed on [public holidays](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/feiertage/).
- [Application and admission periods for degree programmes without an entrance exam procedure](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/)
- [Application and admission period for degree programmes with an entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/deadlines-for-entrance-exam-procedures/)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 March 2026 |
| Rectors's Day (no classes) | 12 March 2026 |
| Easter break | 30 March to 12 April 2026 |
| Whitsun break | 23 May to 25 May 2026 |
| End of classes | 30 June 2026 |
| Lecture-free period (summer break) | 1 July to 30 September 2026 |
| End of semester | 30 September 2026 |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Beginning of semester and classes | 1 October 2026 |
| All Souls Day | 2 November 2026 |
| Christmas break | 21 December 2026 to 6 January 2027 |
| End of classes | 31 January 2027 |
| Lecture-free period (semester break) | 1 to 28 February 2027 |
| End of semester | 28 February 2027 |

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Studying a non-degree programme (again) — Attending individual courses > Deadlines
Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

The start dates of the semesters are: 
- Winter semester: 1 October [1][3][4][5][6]
- Summer semester: 1 March [1][2][3][5][6]

answer:
